title: Sweet-and-Sour Liver
excerpt: Ingredients 600 g lamb liver 90 ml flour 90 ml Bisto gravy powder 10 ml BBQ spice 10 ml BBQ spice seasoning enough oil for shallow frying 500 g tiny baby onions (pickling onions) 375 ml stock 65 ml brown vinegar 15 ml Worcestershire sauce enough sugar to make a sweet-and-sour sauce enough cornflour (Maizena) to …
html:
Ingredients

  • 600 g lamb liver
  • 90 ml flour
  • 90 ml Bisto gravy powder
  • 10 ml BBQ spice seasoning
  • enough oil for shallow frying
  • 500 g tiny baby onions (pickling onions)
  • 375 ml stock
  • 65 ml brown vinegar
  • 15 ml Worcestershire sauce
  • enough sugar to make a sweet-and-sour sauce
  • enough cornflour (Maizena) to thicken the sauce, if needed
  • salt and white pepper to taste
  • 65 ml flat-leaf parsley, roughly chopped

Method

Dip the whole liver in boiling water for a minute, drain and pull off the outer membrane. Cut away any visible fat or sinew. Cut the liver into 3-cm cubes and place in a large bowl.

Mix the flour, Bisto and seasoning in a bowl and sprinkle over the liver. Toss the liver gently so the mixture coats all the surfaces. Shake off the excess flour mixture.

Heat enough oil in a frying pan and fry the liver quickly on all sides. Don't fry the liver too long, otherwise it goes dry – the inside should still be pink. Remove from the pan and set aside.

Fry the onions in the same pan until soft, then add the stock. Cook until the onions are soft, about 10 minutes.

Now add the vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and sugar and let the mixture cook for a minute or so. Taste the sauce – add more vinegar if it's too sweet and more sugar if it's too sour.

Return the liver to the pan once you're happy with the balance of the sauce. Let it cook again for a few minutes or until the liver is done and the sauce has thickened. If not, thicken to taste with a cornflour-and-water mixture.

Season with salt and white pepper and stir through the parsley.

Serves 4 – 6 portions with buttery mashed potatoes.

Easy!
